The '''Belarus national football team''' ({{langx|be|Зборная Беларусі па футболе|Zbornaja Biełarusi pa futbole}}; {{langx|ru|Сборная Беларуси по футболу|Sbornaya Belarusi po futbolu}}) represents [[Belarus]] in men's international [[association football|football]], and is controlled by the [[Football Federation of Belarus]], the governing body for [[football in Belarus]]. Belarus' home ground is [[Dinamo Stadium (Minsk)|Dinamo Stadium]] in [[Minsk]]. Since independence in 1991, Belarus has not yet qualified for a [[FIFA World Cup]] or [[UEFA European Championship]].

==History==
{{Update|section|date=May 2025}}
After the split of the [[Soviet Union]], Belarus played their first match against [[Lithuania national football team|Lithuania]] on 20 July 1992.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_match.php?id=25095|title=Lithuania vs. Belarus|access-date=27 May 2019|website=eu.football.info}}</ref>

Belarus won their group in the [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D]] that contained [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]], [[Moldova national football team|Moldova]], and [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]], and qualified for the country's first-ever playoffs after they finished fourth in their group during [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying]]. The team was scheduled to play against [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]]. However, they lost 1–0, missing a place at UEFA Euro 2020.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Georgia defeats Belarus at UEFA Euro 2020 play-offs |url=https://agenda.ge/en/news/2020/3141|url-status=dead|date=8 October 2020|access-date=17 January 2024|website=Agenda.ge|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201116160441/https://agenda.ge/en/news/2020/3141|archive-date=16 November 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |agency=[[Associated Press]]|date=8 October 2020|title=Georgia beats Belarus, advances to Euro 2020 playoff finals |url=https://www.sandiegouniontribune.com/sports/national/story/2020-10-08/georgia-beats-belarus-advances-to-euro-2020-playoff-finals|access-date=17 January 2024|website=San Diego Union-Tribune}}</ref>

==Team image==
===Nickname===
In August 2016, the Football Federation announced that the team's nickname would be the "White Wings".<ref>{{cite web|url=http://abff.by/index.php?option=com_zoo&task=item&item_id=2988&category_id=2&Itemid=106&lang=ru|script-title=be:Владимир Бережков: "3 сентября приглашаем всех на открытую тренировку сборной"|publisher=Football Federation of Belarus|language=be|date=11 August 2016|access-date=25 August 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181101140017/http://abff.by/index.php?option=com_zoo&task=item&item_id=2988&category_id=2&Itemid=106&lang=ru|archive-date=1 November 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref> The name was influenced by the book {{ill|The Land Beneath White Wings|be|Зямля пад белымі крыламі}} (1977) by Belarusian writer [[Uladzimir Karatkievich]]. The federation's marketing and communications director said: "We are looking at various ways of establishing links with our literary heritage and cultural traditions", stating that "If the Belarusian people opt to associate the team with Karatkevich, almost every phrase in the book can be used as a hashtag!"<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.uefa.org/MultimediaFiles/Download/EuroExperience/uefaorg/Publications/02/39/57/73/2395773_DOWNLOAD.pdf|url-status=dead|title=UEFA Direct {{!}} August/September 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160827081705/http://www.uefa.org/MultimediaFiles/Download/EuroExperience/uefaorg/Publications/02/39/57/73/2395773_DOWNLOAD.pdf|archive-date=27 August 2016}}</ref>

===Home venue===
From September to October 2021, [[Central Stadium (Kazan)|Central Stadium]] in [[Kazan]], Russia has been their home venue because of travel sanctions imposed after an incident with [[Ryanair Flight 4978]].<ref>{{Cite news|last=Blanche |first=Phil|title=Squad withdrawals amid Kazan 'nightmare', Belarus vs. Wales talking points |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/football/wales-belarus-aaron-ramsey-alexander-lukashenko-russia-b1914255.html|newspaper=[[The Independent]]|date=4 September 2021|access-date=17 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Boffey |first=Daniel|title=EU bans Belarus planes from its airspace over activist arrest |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2021/jun/04/eu-bans-belarus-planes-from-its-airspace-over-blogger-raman-pratasevich-arrest|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]|date=4 June 2021|access-date=17 January 2024|issn=0261-3077}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Eccles |first=Mari |title=EU court upholds sanctions against Belarus' airspace regulator over Ryanair plane diversion |url=https://www.politico.eu/article/eu-court-upholds-sanctions-against-belarus-airspace-regulator-over-ryanair-plane-diversion/|website=[[Politico Europe]]|date=15 February 2023|access-date=17 January 2024}}</ref>

However from March 2022 onwards, due to [[Belarusian involvement in the Russian invasion of Ukraine]], [[UEFA]] – the European governing body for [[association football]] – banned Belarusian clubs and national teams from hosting competitive international matches, whilst allowing them to continue playing competitive matches, albeit at a neutral stadium and behind closed doors when they play as host.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Brennan |first=Eliott|title=UEFA bans Belarus from playing international matches at home |url=https://www.insidethegames.biz/articles/1120058/uefa-ban-belarus-international-matches|website=[[Inside The Games]]|date=4 March 2022 |access-date=17 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/10028559-uefa-bars-belarus-from-hosting-international-games-after-invasion-of-ukraine|title=UEFA Bars Belarus from Hosting International Games After Invasion of Ukraine|first=Rob|last=Goldberg|website=[[Bleacher Report]]|date=3 March 2022|access-date=15 May 2025}}</ref><ref name="Belarusian involvement">{{Cite web|title=Belarus teams to play on neutral ground in UEFA competitions |url=https://www.uefa.com/news-media/news/0273-1493944bb9c8-f5773a44dabd-1000--belarus-teams-to-play-on-neutral-ground-in-uefa-competitions/|publisher=[[UEFA]]|date=3 March 2022|access-date=17 January 2024}}</ref> Since then, all of their competitive matches have been held in Hungary or Serbia.

Since June 2025, Belarus play their non-competitive matches in the newly opened [[National Football Stadium (Belarus)|National Football Stadium]] in Minsk<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-06-07 |title=Lukashenko opens National Football Stadium. What is remarkable about it? |url=https://eng.belta.by/president/view/lukashenko-opens-national-football-stadium-what-is-remarkable-about-it-168711-2025/ |access-date=2026-07-04 |website=eng.belta.by |language=en-EN}}</ref>.

==Players==
{{update|date=June 2026}}
===Current squad===

|{{flagicon|Uruguay}} [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to {{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Part of the {{fb|Soviet Union}}''{{efn|Across the Soviet Union's seven [[FIFA World Cup]] appearances, a total of four Belarusian players were included in the squads: Russian born [[Eduard Malofeyev]] ([[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]), [[Sergei Borovsky]] ([[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]), [[Sergei Aleinikov]] ([[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]] and [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]), and [[Andrei Zygmantovich]] ([[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]).}}
|colspan=8|''Part of the {{fb|Soviet Union}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States of America}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''FIFA member from 1992. Not admitted to the tournament.''{{efn|name=omission|FIFA adopted a decision not to allow to participate in the 1994 FIFA World Cup the national teams of those former Soviet republics that did not participate in the qualification draw on 8 December 1991.<ref>{{Cite web|script-title=uk:На переправе|trans-title=At the Crossing|url=http://www.kopanyi-myach.info/index.aspx?page=history11|url-status=dead|website=Kopanyi myach|date=2 December 2015|language=uk|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200814063404/http://www.kopanyi-myach.info/index.aspx?page=history11|archive-date=14 August 2020}}</ref> A proposition of Ukraine to arrange a separate tournament for all successors of the Soviet Union and supported by Georgia and Armenia, was blocked by Russia.<ref>{{Cite web|script-title=uk:Прорубили окно в Америку|trans-title=We hacked window to America|url=http://fanat.ua/national/02.html|url-status=dead|website=Fanat|via=Komanda newspaper|language=uk|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305103050/http://fanat.ua/national/02.html|archive-date=5 March 2016}}</ref>}}
|colspan=8|''Not admitted to the tournament''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]||colspan=8 rowspan=8 |''Did not qualify''
| 6th || 10 || 1 || 1 || 8 || 5 || 21
